scenario: Nimbus Healthcare runs Camunda 8.8 for an admissions platform handling 1200 records per day. A compliance officer asks the operator to permanently retire 1 deprecated process definition version that is no longer referenced. The officer wants a precise list of what will be removed when the operator confirms the Processes-page delete dialog.
question: Which set of artefacts does the userguide say will be impacted when a process definition is deleted from the Processes page?

options.text: Only the BPMN XML for the chosen version is removed from Operate, while finished instance history, related decision instances and any user tasks in Tasklist remain available for at least 90 days for audit

All finished process instances of the deleted definition are removed, related decision and process instances referenced by them are removed, and any user tasks in Tasklist from this definition are deleted
All running process instances are first migrated to the next available version, finished instances are kept as read-only, and Tasklist user tasks are reassigned to a default queue after deletion
Only the deployment record is removed and the underlying instances remain visible under an archived tab in Operate, with Tasklist tasks demoted to claimed-only state pending administrator review

a.text: Incorrect. The note does not preserve finished history or Tasklist tasks for 90 days; finished instances of the deleted definition are removed along with related decision and process instances and any user tasks in Tasklist.

b.text: Correct. The userguide note states that deleting a process definition removes the definition's finished process instances, all decision and process instances referenced by the deleted process instances, and any user tasks that belong to the definition from Tasklist.

c.text: Incorrect. The delete flow is blocked while running instances exist; there is no automatic migration to a next version, and finished instances are removed rather than kept read-only.

d.text: Incorrect. The userguide describes a permanent removal of finished instances and related artefacts, not an archived tab or a demoted Tasklist state.

explanation: The Processes-page delete note lists three permanent impacts of deleting a process definition: the definition's finished process instances are removed, decision and process instances referenced by those deleted process instances are also removed, and any user tasks in Tasklist that belong to the definition are deleted.
